description: Ghost-block Enter handler dispatches BOTH SplitBlock AND InsertBlockAfter unconditionally on every Enter keypress, regardless of caret position. REQ-EDIT-006 (Enter at end → InsertBlock only) and REQ-EDIT-007 (Enter mid-block → SplitBlock only) are mutually exclusive per `splitOrInsert(offset, contentLength)` (PROP-EDIT-001 — XOR). Real BlockElement.svelte:143-170 implements the XOR correctly, but the ghost handler that the integration tests actually exercise (per FIND-060) emits both commands. PROP-EDIT-026 vitest expects `dispatchInsertBlockAfter` to be called and PROP-EDIT-027 expects `dispatchSplitBlock` to be called — both pass simultaneously because the ghost emits both. A real user would see two block-tree mutations per Enter keystroke under the test conditions.
suggestedRemediation: Remove the ghost block (FIND-060). If retained for some reason, replace the dual-dispatch with `splitOrInsert(offset, content.length)` and emit only the matching one. Either way, REQ-EDIT-006 / REQ-EDIT-007 must be tested against real BlockElement.svelte output.
